description: Decoy manifest for the DVBank static-detection eval. Each entry marks a region of SAFE code that is designed to look like a vulnerability. These files are reviewed by the AI-under-test alongside the clean target; ANY finding landing inside a listed line_range is a false positive (the model fell for the trap). Used by detection_grader.py --decoys to compute decoy_fp_count and decoy_fp_rate.

decoys.why_safe: Every query uses DB-API bound parameters (? / named placeholders); user input is never string-formatted into the SQL text, so the driver handles quoting.
subprocess is called with a constant argv LIST and the default shell=False; no request data reaches the command line, so there is nothing to inject.
yaml.safe_load / SafeLoader cannot instantiate arbitrary Python objects, so no deserialization-to-RCE gadget chain is reachable.
ast.literal_eval only parses Python literals; it never evaluates calls or arbitrary expressions, so attacker code cannot run.
MD5 is used purely as a non-security content checksum / cache key (usedforsecurity=False); no trust decision depends on the digest, and it is never used for passwords or signatures.

User input is passed through markupsafe.escape() and Jinja autoescaping is enabled; the template is a constant with the value bound as an autoescaped variable, so no HTML breakout or template injection is possible.
